rulings.Case Title: FIRESIDE THRIFT CO. vs. JONES, MICHAEL L.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Pos And Ntc Of Oppos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 7, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF FIRESIDE THRIFT CO.'s Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The judgment debtor's claim of exemption is granted in part. Judgment debtor shall have $120 withheld per pay period. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BISHOP VS. WYNDHAM WORLDWIDE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Wyndham Vacation Ownership Inc, Trendwest Resorts Inc Notice Of Motion For Orders 1) Compelling Depositions Of Plaintiff'S Experts Prior To May 14, 2012 Trial And 2) Granting Defendants Leave To Depose Experts After The Statutory Close Of Expert Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 7, 2012, Line 17. Defendant Wyndham Vacation Ownership Inc, Trendwest Resorts Inc Notice Of Motion For Orders 1) Compelling Depositions Of Plaintiff'S Experts Prior To May 14, 2012 Trial And 2) Granting Defendants Leave To Depose Experts After The Statutory Close Of Expert Discovery. Denied. Plaintiff was not obligated to produce the witnesses for deposition because Defendants did not serve a new expert witness demand after the Court of Appeal remanded the case. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. There is no indication that plaintiff had cited or otherwise informed defendants of Hirano, the controlling authority on this technical point of procedure, prior to defendants' filing of the motion or that, had defendants known about Hirano, they would have filed their motion. Moreover, all plaintiff needed to do to oppose the motion was to cite Hirano, which should cost far less than $7200 to do. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT DAVID ROBISON VS. CITGO PETROLEUM CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 7, 2012, Line 3. DEFENDANT KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Kelly-Moore Paint Company did not satisfy its initial burden of production. Defendant has not presented sufficient evidence to show that the relation back doctrine does not apply to plaintiff's claims against it. Specifically, Defendant did not present sufficient evidence to demonstrate that plaintiff knew of facts giving rise to a claim against defendant Kelly Moore at the time that plaintiff filed his action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: R. R. VS. EL TRABAJO INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Edward Alkhasov Motion To Reconsider Orders Granting Plaintiff Motions To Compel The Deposition And Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 7, 2012, Line 4, Defendant Edward Alkhasov's Motion To Reconsider Orders Granting Plaintiff Motions To Compel The Deposition And Responses. Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. Moving Defendant did not provide new or different facts, circumstances or law pursuant to C.C.P section 1008. Defendant is ordered to provide responses to form interrogatories, special interrogatories and request for production of documents by May 17, 2012 or terminating sanctions will be considered. To the extent the deposition of plaintiff Mr. Alkhasov's has not been completed, it shall resume by May 21, 2012. Plaintiff's request for Sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL F. KLAPPER VS. GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED, AN ILLINOIS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Case Pending Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 7, 2012, Line 12. DEFENDANT GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Case Pending Arbitration. Denied. Defendant Gentry is not a party to the Sales Representative Agreement and lacks standing to seek arbitration. Per 1281.2(c) there is a substantial risk of inconsistent rulings. It makes little sense to adjudicate claims for indemnity before the underlying tort claims are resolved. Defendant Gentry's untimely arguments regarding the application of Illinois law were not considered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MAHMUT YUKSEL VS. CLAUDIA C BOHORQUEZ et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue And Request For Reasonable Attorney'S Fees And Expenses; Memo Of P&A; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 7, 2012, Line 13. DEFENDANT CLAUDIA BOHORQUEZ'S Motion For Change Of Venue And Request For Reasonable Attorney's Fees And Expenses. Granted as to the request to change venue and denied as to the request for fees and expenses. Notwithstanding that defendant, a member of the bar, failed to document the parties' contract when it was agreed to, the weight of the evidence shows that the parties' oral contract was entered into in Los Angeles County. Palintiff's choice of venue in this county did not lack substantial justification. Any party may appear by telephone through CourtCall. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either email the proposed order to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing or bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MATS ENGSTROM et al VS. TNC HOLDING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 7, 2012, Line 14. DEFENDANT TNC HOLDING, KANBAR SPIRITS, INC., RAMIIILAJ DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first, second, third, seventh, and eighth causes of actions and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action. The standing and jurisdiction arguments lack merit. The first, second, third, seventh, and eighth causes of actions are adequately pled, while the fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action are not. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if leave can do so in good faith, to all of the elements of a fraud claim including justifiable reliance with particularity, their performance or excuse for nonperformance of the allegedly breached contract, and the right to possess particular property at the time that property was converted. While conspiracy is not an independent claim, it will be treated as part of the general charging allegations. The Court declines to take judicial notice of the documents sought to be judicially noticed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LAW OFFICE OF IAN P. ZIMMERMAN APC VS. ASHWIN LADVA et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 7, 2012, Line 15. DEFENDANT ASHWIN LADVA, LADVA LAW FIRM DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all claims. No legally recognizable contract or fiduciary relationship arose as a result of Defendants' knowledge of Plaintiff's lien. Plaintiff is given leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to allege a fiduciary or other relationship of confidence and trust between Plaintiff and Defendants, how and why Defendants owed Plaintiff a duty to disclose, and a contractual relationship upon which the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing is based.
